header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, building programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to bring accurate, detail-obsessed data management and a systematic, human-centered approach to every operational problem.

bullets.text: Audit 300+ leave cases through rigorous {data|data analysis}, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Build executive-ready {data|analyses} on $362K in leave costs, with clean documentation that drove policy redesigns and first aid training
Own recurring {coord|review cycles and operations} for 20+ programs, validating {data|data accuracy} and ensuring deliverables go out on time
Build and maintain {tools|spreadsheet models and tracking systems} in Excel/Google Sheets to monitor performance against plans
Serve as the go-to for inbound questions, triaging promptly and {cross|coordinating with stakeholders} across Marketing, Tech, and Sales
Establish {ex|documentation and SOPs} for 70+ staff, creating scalable, auditable processes and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences with {data|mixed-methods data}, surfacing insights that guided C-suite decisions
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|messy feedback data} into clean, actionable reports
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved process and {ex|experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Managed sensitive compensation and headcount data with discretion across employee relations and succession-planning projects
Optimized onboarding processes for new hires across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying responsibilities
Supported HRBP team by piloting an Inclusive Leadership workshop for senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Strengthened {ex|engagement} via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Researched and modeled global compensation details to inform offers for a challenging international role
Drafted job descriptions, posted openings, and managed recruiting data across HR functions
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
